reason: Implementing a 'deny-all' fapolicyd policy is highly disruptive. While it is a standard hardening pattern, applying it without first creating a comprehensive whitelist of all authorized binaries, libraries, and scripts will immediately break system functionality, prevent service execution, and potentially block administrative tools.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Immediate execution failure of unauthorized or unlisted binaries; Potential breakage of critical system services and cron jobs; Risk of breaking administrative access if shell or system tools are not explicitly permitted
